name: Indian Mackerel
description: A common, medium-sized mackerel that swims in schools and is important for both local consumption and commercial fisheries.
location: Manila Bay, West Philippine Sea (off the coast of Central Luzon)

name: Bangus
description: The national fish of the Philippines and the most important cultured species in the country. Pampanga and Bulacan are major producers.
location: Pampanga, Bulacan

name: Butanding
description: The world's largest fish, known for its gentle nature and filter-feeding habits. Occasionally sighted in offshore waters during migration.
location: Offshore waters of Central Luzon (West Philippine Sea)

name: Dilis
description: Small, schooling fish found near the coast, often used for fish paste and other local dishes. Also known as Bolinao or Tiki-Tiki.
location: Manila Bay, coastal waters of Central Luzon

name: Lapu-Lapu
description: A group of high-value reef fish prized for their firm, white flesh. Found in coral reefs and rocky areas along the coast.
location: Coastal areas and reefs of Central Luzon (e.g., Zambales, Bataan)

name: Maya-Maya
description: Commercially important reef fish with firm, flavorful flesh. Known locally as Maya-Maya and found in reefs off the coast.
location: Coastal areas and reefs of Central Luzon (e.g., Zambales, Bataan)

name: Pawikan
description: These endangered sea turtles nest on beaches and forage in coastal waters. They play a vital role in maintaining marine ecosystem balance.
location: Coastal waters and nesting beaches of Central Luzon (e.g., Zambales, Bataan)

name: Tamban
description: Small, oily, schooling fish that are essential to marine food webs and heavily harvested for commercial use.
location: West Philippine Sea (off the coast of Central Luzon), Manila Bay

name: Milkfish
description: Known as the national fish of the Philippines, bangus is a milkfish species prized for its mild flavor and is extensively farmed in brackish water ponds.
location: Widely cultivated in aquaculture farms across Central Luzon, especially in Pampanga and Bulacan.

name: Tilapia
description: An introduced species that has become a staple in freshwater aquaculture due to its rapid growth and adaptability.
location: Extensively farmed in freshwater ponds and rivers throughout Central Luzon.

name: Mudfish
description: Also known as mudfish, it's valued for its firm meat and is often grilled.
location: Found in rice fields, swamps, and rivers around Central Luzon.

name: Catfish
description: Catfish known for its resilience and is a common ingredient in 'adobo' dishes.
location: Cultivated in fish farms and found in freshwater bodies in the region.

name: Common Carp
description: Common carp introduced for aquaculture purposes, known for its adaptability.
location: Farmed in ponds and found in rivers throughout Central Luzon.

name: Snakeskin Gourami
description: Known as the snakeskin gourami, it's a hardy fish often found in aquaculture and wild habitats.
location: Inhabits freshwater bodies in the region.

name: Silver Perch
description: Endemic to the Philippines, this silver perch is prized for its delicate flavor and is considered one of the most delicious native freshwater fish.
location: Inhabits lakes and rivers, including those in Central Luzon.

name: Speckled Goby
description: A goby species commonly found in freshwater and brackish environments.
location: Inhabits rivers and estuaries within Central Luzon.

name: Giant Freshwater Prawn
description: Giant freshwater prawns known for their large size and sweet meat.
location: Cultivated and caught in freshwater bodies across Central Luzon.

name: Bighead Carp
description: Also known as bighead carp, it's a fast-growing species introduced for aquaculture.
location: Found in Nabao Lake, Nueva Ecija.

name: Indian Mackerel
description: A popular mackerel species in the Philippines, known for its oily flesh and commonly used in various local dishes.
location: Widely found in coastal waters, including those near Central Luzon.

name: Round Scad
description: A staple in Filipino households, this round scad is favored for its affordability and versatility in cooking.
location: Abundant in the coastal waters off Central Luzon.

name: Frigate Tuna
description: A small tuna species appreciated for its rich flavor, often prepared as 'paksiw' or 'ginataan' in Filipino cuisine.
location: Caught in offshore waters near Central Luzon.

name: Spanish Mackerel
description: Also called Spanish mackerel, it's a sought-after fish for its flavorful meat, often used in 'kilawin'.
location: Inhabits open seas but also caught near the coasts of Central Luzon.

name: Grouper
description: A grouper species favored for its tender meat, commonly used in special occasions.
location: Found in rocky and coral reef areas, sometimes sold in Central Luzon markets.

name: Pompano
description: Prized for its buttery texture, it's often grilled or baked whole.
location: Inhabits coastal and estuarine waters near Central Luzon.

name: Yellowfin Tuna
description: Yellowfin tuna known for its rich taste, often used in sashimi and grilled dishes.
location: Caught in open seas, sometimes landed in ports serving Central Luzon.

name: Squid
description: Squid commonly used in various Filipino dishes.
location: Caught in coastal waters, including areas near Central Luzon.

name: Shrimp
description: Shrimp species that are staples in many local recipes.
location: Farmed and wild-caught in and around Central Luzon.

name: Mud Crab
description: Mud crabs known for their sweet meat, often steamed or cooked in coconut milk.
location: Found in mangroves and estuaries near Central Luzon.

name: Blue Swimming Crab
description: Blue swimmer crabs popular for their tender meat.
location: Caught in coastal areas, including those near Central Luzon.

name: Manila Sea Catfish
description: An endemic marine fish found off the coast of Luzon Island, preferring brackish, marine, and freshwater waters.
location: Inhabits areas from Manila to Bataan, including the coastal regions of Central Luzon.

name: Speckled Goby
description: A small goby species native to the Philippines, often found in freshwater and brackish environments.
location: Common in rivers and estuaries throughout Central Luzon.

name: White Sardine
description: A small pelagic fish known for its silvery appearance, commonly used in dried fish products.
location: Caught in coastal waters, including those near Central Luzon.

name: Bloch's Gizzard Shad
description: A species of shad found in coastal and estuarine waters, often forming large schools.
location: Inhabits brackish waters near river mouths in Central Luzon.

name: Longspine Ponyfish
description: A small, silvery fish known for its bioluminescence, commonly found in coastal waters.
location: Caught in the shallow coastal areas of Central Luzon.

name: Philippine Luminous Roughy
description: A deep-sea fish known for its luminescent properties, part of the slimehead family.
location: Found in deeper offshore waters near Central Luzon.

name: Freshwater Sardine
description: The world's only freshwater sardine, endemic to Taal Lake, but its presence in Central Luzon is due to translocation efforts.
location: Introduced in some freshwater bodies within Central Luzon.

name: Freshwater Trevally
description: A freshwater-adapted trevally species, originally from Taal Lake, known for its firm and flavorful meat.
location: Occasionally found in freshwater systems of Central Luzon due to aquaculture.

name: Rough Triggerfish
description: A species of triggerfish known for its rough skin and unique appearance.
location: Inhabits coastal waters, including those near Central Luzon.

name: Picturesque Dragonet
description: A brightly colored fish of the dragonet family, known for its vibrant patterns
location: Found in coral reef areas, sometimes near the coastal regions of Central Luzon.
